  In chartered polities with merchant-coalition and chartered-estate treasury funding

  above the standard-corridor-work rate at first delivery operates as an
  institutional signal of prior suspicion — readable by civic officials, senior
  hunter-officers, and experienced CF-0024 contractors as evidence that the funder
  coalition knows or suspects the target band to be outside the ordinary raid-and-
  ransom profile, often triggering a parallel re-classification notice on the
  magistrate's second's desk that anticipates the contractor's return-testimony.

# Merchant-Coalition Bounty-Row Premium as Prior-Suspicion Signal

## What the Artifact Implies

Melissa's post-intake observation in the closed-room briefing: *"The merchant-coalition had posted a bounty-row premium at first delivery that the standard corridor-work did not justify, and the magistrate's second had on her desk a re-classification notice the clerk's senior already recognized. They had known, or suspected to the level that reading the ruin confirmed."* The claim reconstructs an INSTITUTIONAL SIGNALING MECHANISM: the merchant-coalition posted an above-baseline premium at the contract's FIRST appearance, which — taken together with civic-watch preparatory paperwork — was legible to experienced readers (senior hunter-officer, magistrate's second, posting-clerk's senior) as prior suspicion. The contractor reads the premium from the posting-wall; officials parallel-process the re-classification notice; the formal recognition arrives only after the ruin-reading confirms. The artifact treats this as background practice, not as Harrowgate-particular — the reference to "first delivery" implies a multi-posting cycle against which premium tiers are compared.

## Why It Fits This World

The Charter-Era chartered polity operates through visible posting infrastructure: tavern notice-boards, lockmaster posting walls, civic posting walls, Charter Hall bounty-rows. CF-0034 commits the infrastructure; CF-0024 commits the economic information-market contractors navigate within it; CF-0028 and CF-0030 commit the polity-asymmetric funder-primacy dynamics. What the artifact adds is an information-channel convention layered on the existing infrastructure: the premium tier itself conveys funder-knowledge independent of the contract-text. This fits Animalia's institutional register — economic information routinely encodes civic suspicion at market-interface points (the tavern-keeper vouch-withdrawal when the joke-threshold is crossed per CF-0036; the caravan-master cargo-refusal per CF-0028; the lockmaster disbursement-window as kin-of-record notification). The premium-as-signal mechanism is another instance of the same Charter-Era pattern: visible commercial practice carrying institutional meaning.

## Narrator Reliability Assessment

Melissa "Threadscar" (CHAR-0002) is a CF-0024 upper-band named-veteran contractor with explicit posting-wall reading competence per her dossier's Institutional Embedding section. She read the Harrowgate premium herself and made the contract-acceptance decision on that basis. The interpretive synthesis — that the premium was a signal rather than standard pricing — she arrives at after the closed-room briefing where officials confirm the prior-suspicion pattern. This synthesis is firsthand (witnessed at Harrowgate) and central to the artifact's procedural account. The mechanism is institutional-convention: she reads the convention against her multi-rotation baseline. No CF commits the mechanism; evidence-breadth is therefore artifact-sole, and soft_canon is required by 6d.1. Scope is regional (chartered-polity venues with the requisite infrastructure); the four non-Charter-Hall regional intake patterns per CF-0024 Regional Asymmetry do not host the premium-signal mechanism in the same way.

## Immediate Consequences

Charter Hall bounty-row postings gain a legible secondary information-channel: premium tier communicates funder-knowledge independent of the contract-text. Experienced CF-0024 contractors screen contracts by premium-against-corridor-baseline before accepting, using the reading to negotiate terms, prepare additional rotation time, or decline. Magistrate's offices and civic-watch parallel-process suspected-case preparation (the re-classification notice) in anticipation of contractor return-testimony — producing the pre-staged response Melissa describes. Merchant-coalition funder coordination practice develops a legible convention: premium tier above baseline requires coalition-level authorization that leaves paper trails in chartered-estate treasury records.

## Longer-Term Consequences

Inter-rotation contractor-veteran reputation networks incorporate premium-tier reading as a professional competency — "reads the wall clean" becomes a recognizable tavern-vouch signal distinct from combat-competence, paralleling the CF-0024 portable-reputation value-store into information-literacy territory. Bardic-circuit contracted-bard narrative-custody (CF-0017 extension per CF-0024) develops a premium-tier-signaling cadence in hunter-return material — the ballad opens with the wall-reading rather than the muster, placing contractor pre-contract decision-making in the narrative frame; this is a register-level shift paralleling but distinct from the CF-0035 "we walked the deep wood three days" opening cadence. Civic-registry precedent accumulates around contested-premium disputes: contractors sue for additional hazard-pay when the coalition's premium tier signaled more than the contract-text disclosed; magistrate-court builds evidentiary convention around premium-tier-as-disclosure-surrogate. A false-premium scam subculture emerges at low acuity on the canal-corridor illegitimate-service rail alongside the existing seven product-lines (CF-0023 through CF-0036) — coordinating parties post artificially-elevated premium tiers to signal false-scope contracts for fraud or recruitment purposes.

## Risks

The primary Rule-4 risk is scope drift: the mechanism must not silently extend into highland clan-elder sanction (different funding and sanction mechanics), drylands caravanserai (rotation-pricing not coalition-pricing), or fen monastic-clinic relay-summoning (no bounty-row venue). Rule-3 firewall: premium-tier reading is ordinary contractor-experience competency, not elite specialist ability — no oracle-reading or legendary-wall-reader bardic inflation. Rule-7 M-17 firewall is the sharpest: premium-as-signal gives LOCAL per-case information to officials and contractors; the mechanism must NOT aggregate to continental prevalence claim. Canon-addition adjudication should preserve the per-case scope explicitly — the sealed-docket civic-suppression under CF-0036 continues to restrict aggregate visibility, and the premium-signaling mechanism is a PRE-protocol information-channel, not an END-RUN around the civic-suppression stabilizer. A useful adjudicative principle: the premium invites closer attention, the ruin-reading confirms, the re-classification notice is the formal recognition; no short-circuit from premium to civic consequence.

## Likely Burden If Accepted

Moderate. `ECONOMY_AND_RESOURCES.md` §Wealth Creation and §Breakage Points gain premium-signaling as a named information-market mechanism paralleling the existing visible-consequences patterns. `INSTITUTIONS.md` §Trade/Guilds gains merchant-coalition coordination practice around premium-tier authorization; §Law/Custom/Judgment gains pre-staged magistrate-office preparation in re-classification notices. `EVERYDAY_LIFE.md` (a) heartland canal-town and analogous clusters gain bounty-row reading texture. `CANON_LEDGER.md` receives new CF with annotations to CF-0024, CF-0028, CF-0030, CF-0034, CF-0036.

## Likely Story Yield

Strong. Story engines activated: a contractor who reads the premium wrong (or reads it right but cannot afford to refuse) and walks into a sealed-docket case he did not prepare for; a merchant-coalition scandal where a magistrate traces premium-tier authorization back to a coalition member who had prior intelligence he did not share with civic-watch; a false-premium recruitment scheme where sectarian sympathizers use inflated postings to lure veterans into engagements with supremacist-doctrine intent; a contractor-veteran who builds rotation reputation by "reading the wall clean" and becomes a tavern-vouch authority in her corridor; a civic-registry case where premium-tier evidence becomes admissible in a hazard-pay dispute years after the original contract.

## Would This Be Better As

`soft_canon` as proposed. Regional scope is intrinsic (CF-0024 chartered-body infrastructure + CF-0028 funder-coalition infrastructure + CF-0034 bounty-row infrastructure all vary by region per GEOGRAPHY §Regional Asymmetries). Promotion to hard_canon would violate 6d.1 evidence-breadth. Demotion to contested_canon would misidentify an ordinary market-information convention as disputed — the convention is widely read by officials and contractors without diegetic dispute.

## Canon Safety Check Trace

**Phase 6a (Invariants)**: All 16 invariants tested; zero violations. AES-1 respected (market-information practice is ordinary-labor register, not heroic reading). AES-2 respected (the ordinary keeps the world honest — posting-wall reading is texture). SOC-3 respected (coin-contract mechanics unchanged). DIS-3 respected (no species-distribution claim).

**Phase 6b (Mystery Reserve)**: M-1 through M-18 checked. **M-17 OVERLAP NOTED** — the premium-signaling mechanism engages M-17 (prevalence of interspecies-cannibal cases) surface because officials and contractors read specific-case signals. PASS: the mechanism is per-case and local; it does NOT aggregate to continental prevalence claim, does NOT generate a published prevalence ledger, does NOT commit a continental-rate figure. Per-case visibility is compatible with M-17's civic-suppression-generated bounded-unknown status — the silence-protocol continues to restrict aggregate publication even as per-case officials read signals at contract-time. Other MR entries: no overlap. M-18 explicitly distinct (premium signal is PRE-protocol-invocation, separate from silence-protocol application itself).

**Phase 6c (Distribution Discipline)**: CFs consulted: CF-0024 (contractor infrastructure), CF-0028 (merchant-coalition funder practice), CF-0030 (funder-primacy convention with polity-asymmetric recognition), CF-0034 (bounty-row infrastructure), CF-0036 (silence-protocol background for the closed-room briefing observation). `why_not_universal` cites CF-0028 Regional Asymmetry on Expedition-Tolerance and CF-0024 Regional Asymmetry on Contractor Licensing across all five regional variants — the mechanism belongs to merchant-coalition-funded bounty-row chartered-polity register, explicitly not to highland clan-elder, drylands caravanserai, fen monastic-clinic, or exclusionary-settlement forms.

**Phase 6d (Diegetic-to-World Laundering)**:
- **6d.1 Evidence-breadth**: artifact is sole source for the specific premium-as-suspicion-signal interpretation → soft_canon required → PASS.
- **6d.2 Epistemic-horizon**: Melissa's CF-0024 rotation-experience includes extensive bounty-wall reading; the premium-against-baseline reading is within ordinary veteran competence. The synthesis with closed-room briefing observations is also firsthand → PASS.
- **6d.3 MR Positional**: Melissa's position gives firsthand access to the signaling mechanism per-case; NOT to M-17 continental prevalence data, M-16 doctrine-antiquity archives, M-18 silence-protocol-antiquity founding documents, or M-14 captain-mythography civic-archive. The signaling she reports is per-case and local → PASS.

**Phase 6f Repairs Applied**: none fired.
